ab: Maaq tu uvaz’azang saak a, takihaan saak Naihumpu. Maaq tu isnava’anin saak a, mulusqu munhaan Huli, takihaanin amin bukzavan miqumis.
ch: 小時候我們家住在內茅埔，上學以後，就搬到了埔里，一直在都市裡生活。
en: When we were young, our family lived in Naihumpu. After I started school, we moved to Puli and have been living in the city ever since.

ab: Maaq tu ihaanin saak zaingaku isnava’an a, isnava’an saak pali’uni Takisilazan miniqumis tu kainqansiapan, maqansiap tu, haiza takihaan Taivangti Takisilazan miniqumis madia makitvaivi tu iniqumisan.
ch: 上了大學以後，我修了一堂關於原住民文化的課程，瞭解到臺灣原住民族有各式各樣豐富的文化。
en: After I went to college, I took a course on aboriginal culture and learned that the aboriginal people in Taiwan have various and rich cultures.

ab: Paaqpu, maaq tu pal’uunin a, muskun saak inaak tuqasnanaz munhaan asang kilim madadaingaz tantungu, asa pinqansiap anak’anak siduq iniqumisan tu kaitaiklasan.
ch: 因此，今年暑假時，我和哥哥回到部落訪問老人家，想要瞭解自己族群的文化。
en: So this summer, my brother and I went back to our tribe to visit the elderly, hoping to understand the culture of our own ethnic group.

ab: Mavau’unin qamisan a madaingaza, qaitu, maszangang talmaiduu’ang sadu’an.
ch: 老人家已經八十歲了，但是看起來還像中年人。
en: The old man is already eighty years old, but he still looks like a middle-aged man.

ab: Singavun zaku tu piaq isiata qalinga qansiapun? Madauzung naipa malasBunun, maqtu amin malasPuut siin malasLipuun pababazbaz.
ch: 我問他會哪些語言，他的布農語說得很流利，也會說閩南語和日本話。
en: I asked him which languages he knew, and he was fluent in Bunun, as well as Hokkien and Japanese.

ab: Maaq tu singavunin a, tudiipin saak maqansiap tu, misnahaan naipa uvaz’azang isnava’an malasLipuun, binanau’az qai Bunun. Maaq tu kuzakuza naipa qai, mapasaqaal madia tu Puut. Paaqpun, madia isiata qansiapun qalinga isbabazbaz.
ch: 我問他才知道，原來他小時候就學日本話，太太是布農族人，而他工作上認識許多閩南家人，所以他會說這麼多語言。
en: I asked him to find out that he learned Japanese when he was a child, his wife is an Bunun, and he knows many Hokkien at work, so he can speak so many languages.

ab: Maaq tu tantungu saam a, madia isia madaingazta isbazbaz pali’uni qanup tu kaitiklasan.
ch: 我們訪談的時候，老人家談論了許多關於狩獵的傳統知識。
en: During our interview, the old man talked about a lot of traditional knowledge about hunting.

ab: Anatupa tu nii a qalinga aminun zaku itqaal tan’a ka, langati makusia saam ima-zingva siza qaningu, namaqtu’ang uqna’un ikmantukun tan’a.
ch: 雖然我們沒有辦法完全聽懂，但是我們有用手機錄影，可以再仔細聆聽。
en: Although we could’t fully understand it, we could record it on our cell phones and listen to it more.

ab: Nii saak matlaz maqansiap anak’anak iniqumisan tu uvavaz’azang, matukaz’av tu anak’anak qai Takisilazan tu miniqumis.
ch: 小時候我不瞭解自己族群的文化，對於自己是原住民有點不好意思。
en: When I was young, I didn’t understand the culture of my ethnic group, and I was a little embarrassed that I was an aborigine.

ab: Maaq tu madia’in inaak qansiapun a, haiza’in iniliskinan tu Bunun saak, makitvaivi tu bunun, haiza’an amin maqtu iskukuniv tu kaitaikladsan.
ch: 瞭解愈多以後，愈覺得自己身為[布農族]人，是很獨特的，也很值得驕傲。
en: The more I know, the more I feel that being a Bunun is unique and something to be proud of.
